platform.name: Arduino
platform.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

assumptions: No prior Arduino account configuration or successful use exists.
The developer has the browser and command-line tools listed by the selected path.

developer_goal: Arduino Cloud accepts the application credentials and returns an access token for hosted API use.

first_success.outcome: Arduino Cloud accepts the application credentials and returns an access token for hosted API use.
first_success.observable_signal: The authentication request returns a bearer access token without an authentication error.

route_selection.surface: Arduino Cloud Application API via API key (hosted)
route_selection.rule: Prefer the official hosted quickstart and cURL when documented; stop at the first observable successful happy-path result.
route_selection.selected: Arduino Cloud Application API via API key (hosted)

candidates.name: Arduino Cloud Application API via API key (hosted)
candidates.reason: It is the specified official path to the first-success boundary.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to an Arduino account and open Arduino Cloud.
Create an API key in Arduino Cloud and copy its client ID and client secret.
Submit the documented client-credentials authentication request to Arduino Cloud.
required_path.observable_result: The authentication endpoint returns an access token.

required_path.action: Confirm that the response contains a bearer access token usable with the Application API.
required_path.observable_result: A non-empty access token is present.

prerequisites.description: An Arduino account with access to Arduino Cloud.
An Arduino Cloud API key and client secret.
An HTTPS client such as cURL.

external_gates.description: The client secret is shown as a sensitive credential and must be retained securely.

platform_outcomes.description: Arduino Cloud accepts the application credentials and returns an access token for hosted API use.

excluded.reason: This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.

sources.title: Authenticate to the Arduino Cloud Application API
evidence_supported: Arduino Cloud accepts the application credentials and returns an access token for hosted API use.
The authentication request returns a bearer access token without an authentication error.

uncertainties.question: Does fresh-account UI wording differ from the documented Authenticate to the Arduino Cloud Application API path?
uncertainties.impact: A human should judge minor navigation differences without moving the first-success boundary.
